title: Fruit Juice on Cotton
timeEstimate: 10–30 minutes (fresh); aged adds a peroxide cycle
stainChemistry: Fruit juice is anthocyanin (grape, berry, cranberry, pomegranate) or carotenoid (orange, mango, carrot) pigment + citric acid + fructose. The anthocyanin bond to cotton is hydrogen-bonded; the sugar creates a sticky residue that, if allowed to dry, traps the pigment against the fiber. Cotton tolerates NSD, acid-side spotters, 3% H₂O₂, and hot wash. Grape and cranberry juice carry the heaviest anthocyanin load; orange and apple are lighter. This is essentially the red-wine pattern at lower concentration.
whyThisWorks: Same tannin sequence: cold flush, NSD for sugar + surface pigment, acid-side spotter for anthocyanin hydrogen bond, 3% H₂O₂ for residual chromophore, hot wash. Dark-anthocyanin juices may need an extra peroxide cycle; light carotenoid juices often clear in one pass.
defaultAssumption: Most juice spills are kid-centric (apple, orange, grape). Fresh = high-confidence DIY. Dark-anthocyanin juices on light cotton = two cycles. Heat-set = pro.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Cold water, white cotton terry towel
spottingProtocol.instruction: Cold flush from the BACK of the fabric. Most fresh juice lifts here — sugar and pigment are water-soluble while fresh.
Apply NSD, sponge gently. Handles residual sugar + pigment.
Under 30 min old only. Hot pour-through from the back. Skip on aged stains.

spottingProtocol.equipment: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — acid-side tannin treatment
spottingProtocol.instruction: Acid-side spotter by dropper on residual color. Dwell 3–5 min, blot. Skip for fresh light-juice.
3% H₂O₂ on residual chromophore. Skip on dark cotton.
Rinse until water runs clear. Hot wash per care label.

steps: 4. Aged / dark juice: acid-side tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter / PRENETT-A).
6. NSD flush, hot wash per care label.
products: Professional tannin spotter (Chemspec Tannin Spotter or PRENETT-A) — aged / dark only
professionalProtocol.temperature: cold through spotting; hot wash post-chemistry
warnings: Never use hot water on aged juice — sets tannin
Dark juice on light cotton = budget 2 cycles

homeSolutions.instruction: Fresh juice on cotton comes out easily. Hold the BACK of the stained area under cold running water for 30–60 seconds. Cold only.
If the spill is under 30 minutes old, stretch the fabric over a bowl and pour hot (not boiling) water through from the back. Skip for aged stains — sets the color.
Mix 1 tsp Dawn + 1 tbsp white vinegar + 1 cup cold water. Dab on the spot, let sit 3 min, blot from center outward. Repeat once if color remains.

homeSolutions.agent: OxiClean or 3% Peroxide for Stubborn Shadow
homeSolutions.instruction: Dissolve 1 scoop OxiClean in 1 cup cool water, soak stained area 15–30 min. Or 3% peroxide on spot for whites 3–5 min. Skip on dark cotton.
homeSolutions.safetyNote: Never mix OxiClean with chlorine bleach, ammonia, or hydrogen peroxide.

homeSolutions.agent: Hot Wash, Inspect Before the Dryer
homeSolutions.instruction: Launder hot per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.
If juice has been hot-dried or two rounds don't clear, take to a dry cleaner.

steps: 4. OxiClean soak OR 3% peroxide on residual. Never layer oxidizers.
5. Hot wash per care label. Inspect BEFORE the dryer.
diyProtocol.whenToStop: Stop if juice has been hot-dried and home round doesn't clear it.
warnings: Hot water only for fresh — sets aged tannin
Never mix OxiClean with chlorine bleach or hydrogen peroxide

neverDo: Never use hot water on aged juice — sets tannin
fiberSensitivities: Cotton tolerates NSD, acid-side spotters, 3% H₂O₂, hot wash

escalation.when: Heat-set by iron or dryer, or two home rounds plateau.
escalation.whatToTell: "Fruit juice on cotton — [type], age [fresh / overnight / older], home-treated with Dawn + vinegar + OxiClean / 3% peroxide."

contextQuestions: Which juice — grape / cherry / cranberry (heavy anthocyanin) or orange / apple (lighter)?
Has it been hot-washed or dryer-cycled?
Is the cotton white, light, or dark/dyed?
deepSolveHooks.modifierNotes: Dark-anthocyanin juices (grape, blackcurrant, cranberry) carry 3–4× the pigment load of orange or apple — budget accordingly.
